title: noamseg/interview-coach-skill
summary: This is an AI-powered interview coach built on Claude Code, designed to guide users through the entire job search lifecycle, from resume optimization and mock interviews to post-offer negotiation. It offers adaptive coaching, detailed scoring, and personalized feedback to improve interview performance.
risk_notes: The installation method is not explicitly stated, but it's a Claude Code-based tool, implying it likely requires a Claude API key. Users are prompted to share their resume, which contains sensitive personal data, and this data is processed and stored locally in a `coaching_state.md` file, raising privacy considerations. The listed creation and last push dates (2026) are in the future, which is highly unusual and could indicate an error in the repository metadata; assuming these are typos, the project appears comprehensive and well-described.

title: caamer20/telegram-drive
summary: Telegram Drive is an open-source desktop application that transforms your Telegram account into an unlimited, secure cloud storage solution, offering a file explorer interface for managing files on Telegram's servers.
risk_notes: The desktop app (Windows, macOS, Linux) is built and signed automatically by GitHub CI and features auto-updates, which is a good sign for maintenance and trust. It requires Telegram API credentials, but states that API keys and data stay local with no third-party servers. A local REST API for AI integration is off by default and requires API key authentication. The Android version is an unsigned APK requiring 'Install from Unknown Sources' and contains Google AdMob banner ads, which introduces a higher risk profile for mobile users. The project is actively maintained with recent pushes.

title: secure-77/perlite
summary: A web-based markdown viewer optimized for Obsidian notes, offering features like auto-building, Obsidian theme support, and a graph view. It can be self-hosted.
risk_notes: Installation involves downloading releases or using Docker. The project mentions security considerations regarding direct access to markdown files and metadata.json, recommending that only the PHP engine should have access. The last commit was in January 2026, which is in the future, suggesting a potential issue with the timestamp or a very active development cycle. However, the 'Last push' date in the repo metadata is 2026-01-21, which is in the future. This is likely an error in the metadata provided, but it's worth noting. Assuming it's a typo and the project is actively maintained based on other indicators like open issues and recent commits.

title: ddlmanus/macoptimizer
summary: MacOptimizer is a macOS system optimization and app management tool built with SwiftUI. It offers features like app uninstallation, junk cleaning, system monitoring, large file finding, and file management.
risk_notes: Installation is via DMG download from GitHub releases, which is a relatively safe method. The tool requires access to the filesystem to scan for junk files, application residuals, and manage files. It also monitors system processes. No network access or credentials are explicitly mentioned as required. The project appears actively maintained with a recent push and open issues.

title: seeed-studio/oshw-recamera-series
summary: reCamera is a series of open-source AI cameras powered by RISC-V SoCs, offering on-device AI performance and modular hardware for vision AI systems.
risk_notes: This repository focuses on hardware specifications and product series for AI cameras. Installation and usage details are not provided in the truncated README, but it appears to be a hardware product with associated software development. Security posture would depend on the specific software and firmware provided with the hardware, which is not detailed here. The project is marked as actively maintained with a recent push date.

title: asimovinc/asimov-v0
summary: An open-source project for building bipedal robotic legs for humanoid robots, using off-the-shelf components and compatible with low-volume manufacturing.
risk_notes: This repository focuses on hardware design for a humanoid robot's legs. It does not appear to be a software tool, CLI, or automation project relevant to the user's stated interests in developer tools, self-hosting, AI/LLM tooling, or Raspberry Pi projects. Installation and usage would involve physical construction and integration, not typical software deployment.

title: zackriya-solutions/meeting-minutes
summary: A privacy-first, self-hosted AI meeting assistant that provides real-time transcription and summarization using local processing on macOS and Windows.
risk_notes: Installation is via a standard installer for Windows (.exe) and a disk image for macOS (.dmg), which is a relatively safe method. It processes audio and text locally, but it does have the capability to connect to various AI providers (Ollama, Claude, Groq, OpenAI-compatible endpoints) for summarization, which would involve network access and potentially API keys. The project appears to be actively maintained with recent pushes and a good number of stars, and it's open-source with an MIT license.

title: kousen/claude-code-training
summary: A training course repository providing slides, lab exercises, and instructor guides for using Claude Code in professional software development workflows, covering topics like code generation, refactoring, and automation.
risk_notes: Requires Claude Code installation and an Anthropic API key. Installation of the presentation tool (Slidev) uses `pnpm install` and running it uses `pnpm dev`. No direct system access is implied beyond what Claude Code itself requires. The project appears to be actively maintained with a recent last push date.

title: going-doer/paper2code
summary: Paper2Code is an LLM system that automates code generation from scientific papers in machine learning, transforming a paper into a functional code repository through a multi-agent pipeline.
risk_notes: Installation involves `pip install` for dependencies like `openai` or `vllm`. It requires an OpenAI API key or configuration for open-source models. The script `run.sh` or `run_llm.sh` is executed from the `scripts` directory. It processes paper content (PDF or LaTeX) and generates code, potentially accessing local files for input and writing to the filesystem for output. No obvious security risks beyond standard API key management.

title: dembrandt/dembrandt
summary: A CLI tool that extracts design system tokens (logo, colors, typography, etc.) from any website into a structured format, with support for AI agent integration and drift tracking.
risk_notes: Installs via npm (`npm install -g dembrandt` or `npx`). Requires Node.js. Primarily interacts with websites over the network. Optional API key for cloud sync/drift tracking, which would grant access to the service's backend. Actively maintained with recent pushes and active development indicated by AI agent integration and app features.

title: pyrochlore/obsidian-tracker
summary: An Obsidian plugin that tracks and visualizes occurrences and numbers within your notes, offering various charting and data processing capabilities.
risk_notes: Installation is via Obsidian's plugin system, which is generally safe. It does not require elevated permissions or network access beyond what Obsidian itself needs. The project appears actively maintained with recent updates, though the last push date in the provided info (2026-04-13) seems to be in the future and might be a data error; the actual last commit date should be verified. The open issues count (217) is relatively high, which might indicate potential stability or feature request backlogs.

title: guy-hartstein/company-research-agent
summary: An agentic company research tool powered by LangGraph and Tavily, using a multi-agent framework with Gemini and OpenAI models to conduct deep diligence on companies.
risk_notes: Requires multiple API keys (Tavily, Google Gemini, OpenAI, Google Maps) which need to be managed securely. Installation involves cloning the repo and running a setup script (`setup.sh`) which installs Python and Node.js dependencies. The script does not use `curl | sudo bash` but does require environment variables to be set for API keys. The project appears actively maintained with a recent push date and a low number of open issues. It runs as a web service (FastAPI backend, React frontend) and does not require elevated system privileges beyond what's needed for API key access.

title: cporter202/awesome-ai-tools
summary: A curated list of AI tools for building, automating, and monetizing projects, covering content creation, video automation, APIs, websites, workflows, and AI voice.
risk_notes: This repository is a curated list of external tools and services, not a piece of software to be installed directly. The risk is associated with the individual tools listed, which may require API keys, subscriptions, or have their own security postures. One tool, Postiz, is noted as open-source and self-hostable, which could mitigate some risks if implemented correctly. The repository itself has no installation or permission requirements.

title: aouicher/graphmind
summary: GraphMind is a local-first tool that transforms codebases into queryable knowledge graphs for AI assistants, aiming to improve AI's understanding of code structure and reduce token usage.
risk_notes: Installation via curl script, Homebrew, or direct download. The desktop app is for macOS only. It requires access to the local filesystem to index codebases and integrates with AI assistants, potentially accessing their configurations and data. No network access is required by default, and no telemetry is mentioned. The project appears actively maintained with recent pushes and CI checks.

title: joylarkin/awesome-ai-market-maps
summary: An Awesome List curating over 500 AI market maps from 2025 and 2026, organized by quarter and topic, with contributions accepted via pull requests or direct contact.
risk_notes: This repository is a curated list and does not involve direct installation or execution of code. It links to external resources and data. The primary interaction is through viewing the list, searching a CSV, or exploring datasets on Hugging Face. No direct security risks are associated with this repository itself.

title: 0xranx/opencontext
summary: OpenContext is a personal context store for AI agents and assistants, allowing users to capture, search, and reuse project knowledge across different coding agents and repositories via a CLI and a desktop GUI.
risk_notes: Installation is via npm, which is a standard package manager. The tool integrates with AI coding agents (Codex, Claude, OpenCode) and requires configuration files to be placed in user directories (~/.cursor, ~/.claude, ~/.codex). It has network access for agent communication and filesystem access for managing context data. The last push was relatively recent, indicating active maintenance.

title: arcangel0/eva
summary: EVA is an AI-assisted penetration testing agent designed to enhance offensive security workflows by providing structured attack guidance, contextual analysis, and integration with multiple AI backends.
risk_notes: Installation involves pip or cloning the repo and running a Python script. It requires configuration for API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, etc.) or Ollama for local models. The tool interacts with external AI services and potentially performs actions on the target system during a penetration test, which could have security implications if misused. The project appears actively maintained with a recent push.

title: sgoudelis/ground-station
summary: A comprehensive, browser-based ground station suite for satellite tracking, SDR signal reception, and telemetry decoding.
risk_notes: The project is actively maintained and provides a modern web interface for hardware control. It requires access to radio hardware (SDRs) and likely needs to run with sufficient permissions to interface with USB/SoapySDR drivers. It uses SQLite for data storage and includes authentication, suggesting it should be deployed in a secure, isolated network environment rather than exposed directly to the public internet.

title: leigest519/opengame
summary: OpenGame is an agentic framework designed to generate end-to-end web games from natural language prompts, utilizing a specialized LLM (GameCoder-27B) and an evaluation pipeline for build health and usability.
risk_notes: Requires Node.js 20+. As an agentic framework, it likely executes generated code locally, which poses significant security risks if the generated code is malicious or contains vulnerabilities. It requires API keys for the underlying LLM and should be run in a sandboxed or isolated environment to prevent unauthorized filesystem or network access.

title: cloudflare/agentic-inbox
summary: A self-hosted, AI-integrated email client built entirely on Cloudflare's serverless stack, utilizing Durable Objects for storage and Workers AI for agentic capabilities.
risk_notes: Requires a Cloudflare account and domain. Deployment is handled via Wrangler CLI. Security relies on Cloudflare Access (JWT validation) for authentication; the application architecture assumes a single trust boundary where any user authorized by the Access policy has full control over all mailboxes and the MCP server.

title: blockrunai/clawrouter
summary: An agent-native, open-source LLM router that enables autonomous agents to access AI models via wallet-based authentication and USDC micropayments, bypassing traditional API key and credit card requirements.
risk_notes: Installation involves a curl-to-shell script, which requires caution. The tool is designed to manage AI model access via local wallet signatures and crypto-based payments; it requires network access to communicate with model providers and blockchain networks. It is actively maintained but introduces dependency on the x402 protocol and crypto-wallet management.

title: psalias2006/gpu-hot
summary: A lightweight, self-hosted real-time dashboard for monitoring NVIDIA GPU metrics, supporting both single-node and multi-node cluster aggregation via Docker.
risk_notes: Requires Docker and the NVIDIA Container Toolkit. Running with --pid=host for process monitoring grants the container visibility into host-level processes, which is a standard but elevated permission for monitoring tools. The project is actively maintained and uses standard Docker deployment patterns.

title: internscience/graphgen
summary: GraphGen is a sophisticated framework for generating synthetic, knowledge-graph-guided training data for LLMs, designed to improve fine-tuning performance through targeted, high-value data synthesis.
risk_notes: Installed via pip. Requires significant local compute resources (GPU/RAM) and likely API keys for LLM providers or local inference backends (vLLM, Ollama). It processes local files (PDFs, datasets) and interacts with external databases (NCBI, Wikipedia), so it requires standard filesystem and network access. Actively maintained with frequent updates.

title: nicholasaleks/damn-vulnerable-drone
summary: An intentionally vulnerable drone hacking simulator using ArduPilot and MAVLink, designed for security professionals to practice offensive techniques in a safe, Docker-based environment.
risk_notes: The project is containerized using Docker, which provides good isolation from the host system. It requires significant system resources and potentially network access to simulate wireless interfaces. As it is an intentionally vulnerable environment, it should be run in an isolated network segment to prevent accidental exposure of the host machine.

title: researai/awesome-ai-scientist
summary: A comprehensive curated list of research papers, frameworks, and tools focused on AI-driven scientific discovery and research automation.
risk_notes: This is a documentation/curation repository and contains no executable code itself. It links to external web services and third-party repositories. Users should exercise caution when running external AI agent code found in the linked projects, as they may require API keys (OpenAI/Anthropic) and have access to local filesystems or network resources.

title: buzhangsan/skill-manager
summary: A management tool for discovering and installing community-contributed skills for AI coding assistants like Claude Code and Antigravity.
risk_notes: Requires manual installation by copying files into local agent directories (~/.claude/skills/). It executes JavaScript code within the context of your AI agent, which may have broad filesystem or network access depending on the agent's configuration. The project lacks a formal license file despite claiming MIT in the README, and it relies on a large static JSON database of community skills.

title: robzilla1738/supergoal
summary: An autonomous task-planning and execution framework for Claude Code and Codex CLI that decomposes complex software tasks into multi-phase, self-healing workflows.
risk_notes: Installs as a plugin for Claude Code or via manual file copy to ~/.claude/skills. It requires full read/write access to your local codebase and filesystem to perform autonomous builds. It relies on the security posture of the host CLI (Claude Code/Codex) and executes generated code, so it should only be used in trusted environments.

title: matro7sh/bypassav
summary: A comprehensive mind-map and knowledge base detailing techniques for bypassing anti-virus and EDR solutions, intended for security research and red teaming.
risk_notes: This is a documentation-only repository containing markdown and mind-map files. There is no executable code to install, making it safe to browse. It provides educational information on security bypass techniques.

title: xxm19/hommi
summary: A specialized research repository for training mobile manipulation robots using human demonstrations, specifically tailored for the RBY1 robot platform.
risk_notes: Requires complex environment setup via mamba/conda and specific hardware dependencies (RBY1 robot). It involves running custom Python code for hardware control and data processing, which requires local filesystem access and potentially network access for camera streams. It is a highly niche research project rather than a general-purpose developer tool.

title: achannarasappa/ticker
summary: A highly configurable terminal-based TUI for tracking stock and cryptocurrency prices, positions, and market data in real-time.
risk_notes: The tool requires network access to fetch financial data from public APIs. It can be installed via Homebrew, Snap, or pre-compiled binaries. The manual Linux install script uses sudo to move the binary to /usr/local/bin, which is a standard but privileged operation. It reads a local YAML configuration file for user positions and watchlists; no sensitive API keys are explicitly required as it relies on public market data.

title: ramziddin/solid-skills
summary: A collection of AI agent skills designed to enforce SOLID principles, TDD, and clean architecture patterns in TypeScript and NestJS projects.
risk_notes: Installed via npx, which executes remote code in the local environment. As an AI agent skill, it requires integration with an agent framework (like Anthropic's) and will have read/write access to your codebase and potentially your LLM API keys. The project is very new and lacks a formal license file despite the README claim.

title: cert-manager/cert-manager
summary: The industry-standard tool for automating the management, issuance, and renewal of TLS certificates within Kubernetes clusters.
risk_notes: Installs as a set of controllers within a Kubernetes cluster, typically via Helm or YAML manifests. It requires high-level cluster permissions (ClusterRole) to manage secrets and ingress resources. It is a CNCF project with high security standards, but it handles sensitive private keys and interacts with external certificate authorities (like Let's Encrypt), requiring careful configuration of RBAC and secret management.

title: 2929004360/ruoyi-qs-nvr
summary: A comprehensive enterprise-grade NVR system built on the RuoYi-Cloud framework, supporting a wide range of protocols including GB28181, JT808, and RTSP/RTMP.
risk_notes: Requires a complex stack including MySQL, Redis, and Nacos. Installation involves deploying multiple microservices, which increases the attack surface. It handles sensitive video streams and requires network access to camera hardware, so it should be isolated in a VLAN. Maintenance appears active, but the complexity of the RuoYi ecosystem requires significant operational overhead.

title: nvchad/nvchad
summary: A highly optimized, modular Neovim configuration framework written in Lua, designed for extreme performance and aesthetic customization.
risk_notes: Installed by cloning the repository into the local Neovim configuration directory (~/.config/nvim). It executes arbitrary Lua code and manages plugins that may access the filesystem, network (for LSP/language servers), and environment variables. Requires manual review of plugins and configuration files for security.

title: pacifio/ui
summary: Atlas is a framework-agnostic design language and UI system specifically optimized for AI coding agents to generate consistent, AMOLED-black interfaces.
risk_notes: The project is lightweight and low-risk. It can be installed as a Claude Code skill, via git clone, or by downloading a single CSS file. It does not require root access, API keys, or persistent background processes. It is a design system/CSS reference, so it only impacts the visual output of your generated code.

title: elinglijiaoqiao/presentation-ppt-maker
summary: A specialized multi-agent pipeline designed to convert academic papers into structured, high-quality PowerPoint presentations using Claude Code skills.
risk_notes: The tool is installed as a Claude Code skill by symlinking the directory into ~/.claude/skills/. It requires access to your local filesystem to read PDFs and write PPTX files. It relies on the Claude Code environment and its associated API keys/permissions. The code is very new (created June 2026) and lacks a significant community track record, so it should be audited for file system access patterns.

title: maddog241/mvinverse
summary: MVInverse is a research-oriented framework for feed-forward multi-view inverse rendering, enabling rapid reconstruction of scene geometry and material properties from image sequences.
risk_notes: Standard Python/PyTorch installation via pip. Requires significant GPU resources (CUDA) and downloads model weights from Hugging Face. The code executes local scripts on provided image directories, so it has full read/write access to the specified filesystem paths.

title: agentphone-ai/agentphone-mcp
summary: An MCP server that enables AI agents to manage phone numbers, send SMS, and conduct voice calls via natural language commands.
risk_notes: Requires an API key from a third-party service (agentphone.ai) to function. Installation is via npx, which executes remote code. The server acts as a bridge to a proprietary API, meaning it requires network access to that service and handles sensitive communication data (SMS/calls).

title: axiom0x0/bifrost
summary: A lightweight, zero-config CLI tool for transferring files between a computer and a mobile device over a local network using QR codes and browser-based Web Crypto API encryption.
risk_notes: Distributed as a single binary; requires network access to host an HTTP server on the local LAN. The install method involves downloading a binary and moving it to /usr/local/bin, which is standard but requires sudo. It accesses the local filesystem to serve or receive files. The project is very new (created April 2026), so long-term maintenance is unproven.

title: d3ext/wef
summary: A comprehensive bash-based framework for Wi-Fi security auditing and penetration testing, supporting a wide range of WEP, WPA, and WPS attack vectors.
risk_notes: Requires root privileges to execute as it interacts directly with network interfaces and performs packet injection. Installation involves cloning a repository and executing a bash script, which may trigger automatic dependency installation via system package managers. Use only in authorized testing environments as it is designed for offensive security operations.

title: jacob-bd/perplexity-web-mcp
summary: A powerful CLI and MCP server that enables access to Perplexity AI's premium models and research capabilities via an unofficial API wrapper.
risk_notes: Installs via pip/uv. It relies on unofficial, undocumented web interface scraping, which carries a risk of account suspension by Perplexity. It requires authentication credentials for your Perplexity account and provides broad access to your AI model quotas and search history.

title: afaanbilal/nanocore
summary: NanoCore is a minimalist 8-bit CPU emulator written in Rust, featuring a custom instruction set, an assembler, and a TUI debugger for educational purposes.
risk_notes: Installed via 'cargo install' or built from source, which is standard for Rust projects. It runs as a local user process with no special permissions; it only accesses files provided as arguments (assembly or binary files). The project is actively maintained.

title: dryxio/auto-re-agent
summary: An autonomous reverse-engineering agent that integrates with Ghidra to automate code reversal, featuring a parity engine for heuristic verification and an objective verifier to ensure structural accuracy.
risk_notes: Requires Python 3.10+ and installation via pip. It interacts with local binaries and Ghidra project files, requiring read/write access to the filesystem. It relies on external LLM APIs (Anthropic/OpenAI) or local CLI tools, necessitating API keys or local credentials. The tool is highly active and designed for local execution without auto-commit features, which mitigates some risk.

title: mushibo/micro-wheeled_leg-robot
summary: An open-source, desktop-sized two-wheeled-leg robot project featuring custom PCB designs, 3D-printable mechanical parts, and ESP32-based firmware using SimpleFOC.
risk_notes: This is a hardware project requiring custom PCB fabrication, 3D printing, and assembly. Software is flashed to an ESP32 via Arduino IDE. The device hosts a local web server (WebSocket) for control; it does not require external cloud access, but users should be aware of the security implications of running local network-connected hardware.

title: harimxchoi/google-surf-mcp
summary: A powerful Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides Google search, URL fetching, and academic PDF extraction capabilities without requiring API keys.
risk_notes: Installs via npm (npx), which is standard for Node.js projects. It requires a local installation of Google Chrome or Chromium and creates a persistent browser profile directory (~/.google-surf-mcp). It performs web scraping, which involves network access to external sites and potential interaction with CAPTCHAs. It does not require API keys, reducing credential exposure risks, but users should be aware that it runs browser automation locally on their machine.

title: pozzettiandrea/comfyui-trellis2
summary: A ComfyUI custom node implementation for Microsoft's TRELLIS.2 model, enabling high-quality image-to-3D mesh generation within local AI workflows.
risk_notes: Installs as a custom node within ComfyUI. The project uses an experimental 'comfy-env' wrapper and the 'pixi' package manager for dependency isolation. Requires significant local GPU resources and access to the filesystem for model weights and output generation. Standard Python dependency management via pip is also supported.

title: ttktjmt/mjswan
summary: A framework for running MuJoCo physics simulations and reinforcement learning policies directly in the browser using WebAssembly and ONNX Runtime.
risk_notes: This is a client-side library (JavaScript/TypeScript) intended for browser execution. It does not require system-level installation or elevated privileges. It relies on standard web technologies (Three.js, ONNX Runtime) and is safe to use in a browser sandbox. Maintenance appears active with recent updates.

title: growthenginenowoslawski/coldoutboundskills
summary: A collection of Claude Code skills designed to automate the end-to-end cold email lifecycle, from domain infrastructure setup and lead sourcing to copywriting and campaign management.
risk_notes: Requires cloning the repository and providing multiple sensitive API keys (Smartlead, Prospeo, Dynadot, etc.) in a .env file. The project is designed to be executed via Claude Code, which grants the AI agent broad filesystem access and the ability to execute arbitrary scripts on your machine. Users should review the TypeScript code in each skill before execution to ensure the agent's actions align with their security expectations.

title: pranshuchittora/simvyn
summary: A powerful, unified dashboard and CLI tool for managing iOS simulators, Android emulators, and physical mobile devices, offering features like log streaming, file management, and location simulation.
risk_notes: Installed via npm (npx or global install). It requires access to local device management tools (Xcode/simctl, Android SDK/adb) and interacts with connected physical devices. It runs a local web server to provide the dashboard interface. As a developer tool, it operates with the permissions of the user running the CLI, which is standard for this type of utility.

title: agricidaniel/automated-business-analysis-workflow
summary: An n8n workflow template that automates website scraping via Firecrawl and AI-driven business analysis using Gemini/Perplexity, outputting results to Google Workspace.
risk_notes: Requires self-hosting or cloud-hosting of n8n. Accesses sensitive user data via Google Drive/Docs/Gmail API scopes and requires multiple third-party API keys (Firecrawl, Perplexity, Gemini). Security depends entirely on the n8n instance configuration and the safety of the provided API credentials.

title: qoyyuum/mcp-metatrader5-server
summary: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that enables AI assistants to interact with the MetaTrader 5 trading platform for market analysis and automated trading operations.
risk_notes: Requires installation via uv or pip. The server interacts directly with the MetaTrader 5 terminal on Windows, meaning it has the potential to execute financial trades and access account credentials. Users should exercise extreme caution when granting an LLM control over trading functions. It is actively maintained and uses standard Python packaging practices.

title: aiksa2090/agentic-swarm
summary: A local, offline-first multi-agent AI system built with PySide6 and PyTorch that orchestrates five specialized agents for collaborative brainstorming using Gemma-4 models.
risk_notes: Requires local execution of Python scripts and heavy dependencies (PyTorch, CUDA). It accesses the local filesystem to load large model weights from HuggingFace. It requires significant hardware resources (12GB+ VRAM). The installation involves running custom PowerShell scripts and downloading weights from external repositories, which should be audited before execution.

title: realmistic/stocks-scoring-agent
summary: A comprehensive AI agent framework for financial analysis that integrates SEC filings, market data, and social sentiment using both OpenAI and local Ollama models.
risk_notes: Requires API keys for OpenAI and SEC (via email identification), plus optional Polygon API keys. Uses 'uv' for dependency management, which is a modern and safe Python packaging tool. The code executes external API calls and potentially processes sensitive financial data; users should review the agent logic before running automated trading or analysis scripts.

title: showlab/code2video
summary: Code2Video is an academic research project that generates educational videos by executing code, offering a more precise alternative to traditional video-generation models for technical content.
risk_notes: This is a research-oriented Python repository. It likely requires significant local compute resources (GPU) and dependencies. As with most AI research code, it may execute arbitrary code generated by LLMs to produce video output, posing a potential security risk if run on untrusted inputs. Standard pip installation is expected, but users should review the execution pipeline for sandbox limitations.

title: doble-2/osint-d2
summary: An agentic OSINT toolkit that automates identity correlation, breach analysis, and cognitive profiling using LLMs and proxy-backed scraping.
risk_notes: Requires Python 3.11+ and Poetry for installation. The tool requires multiple external API keys (AI providers and ScrapingAnt) and performs network-intensive scraping operations. It handles sensitive data (PII/breach info) and requires local system dependencies for PDF generation. Users should be aware that it executes LLM-driven autonomous agents, which may perform unpredictable network requests.

title: eliautobot/my-virtual-office
summary: A self-hosted 2D visual dashboard that provides a physical office interface for monitoring and interacting with various AI agent frameworks like Claude Code, Codex, and Hermes.
risk_notes: Distributed as a Docker container. Requires mounting sensitive host directories (agent home directories, config paths) into the container to function. It interacts with local CLI tools (Claude Code, Codex, Hermes) and can execute commands on the host if configured with high-privilege sandbox modes (e.g., danger-full-access). Requires careful configuration of environment variables to isolate credentials and workspace files.

title: johnriceml/clawport-ui
summary: A visual dashboard and command center for managing AI agent teams built on the OpenClaw framework, featuring kanban boards, cost tracking, and live log monitoring.
risk_notes: Requires installing OpenClaw via a curl-to-bash script, which carries inherent security risks. The dashboard runs locally and connects to an OpenClaw gateway on localhost, requiring read access to your local workspace files and agent memory. It is actively maintained but relies on the OpenClaw ecosystem.

title: espressif/esp-claw
summary: An AI agent framework by Espressif designed to run locally on ESP32-series microcontrollers, enabling IoT devices to perform edge decision-making and event-driven automation.
risk_notes: Requires flashing firmware onto ESP32 hardware via browser-based WebSerial or local ESP-IDF build tools. The framework interacts with external LLM APIs (OpenAI/Anthropic/DeepSeek), requiring API keys to be configured on the device. As an edge agent, it can be configured to control local hardware peripherals, so security depends on the user's implementation of local logic and network isolation.

title: kmx415/meshpoint
summary: A powerful Raspberry Pi-based base station for Meshtastic networks using SX1302/SX1303 concentrators, featuring a full web dashboard for messaging, node management, and packet analysis.
risk_notes: Requires hardware-level access to GPIO pins for the LoRa concentrator. The software runs on Raspberry Pi and likely requires root or specific group permissions to interface with SPI/I2C and network interfaces. It persists data in a local SQLite database and includes an optional cloud uplink to a third-party service (Meshradar). Users should review the configuration files for sensitive PSK storage.

title: interviewready/system-design-resources
summary: A comprehensive, curated collection of high-quality engineering blog posts and technical resources focused on distributed systems, architecture, and scalable design patterns.
risk_notes: This is a documentation-only repository containing links to external articles. There is no code to execute, no installation process, and no security risk associated with the repository itself.

title: johannhotzel/molecules
summary: A GPU-accelerated particle physics simulation framework built for the Unity game engine using HLSL compute shaders.
risk_notes: This is a Unity project asset. It requires importing into a Unity project and running within the Unity Editor. It does not perform external network calls or require system-level permissions, but it executes custom HLSL compute shaders on the GPU, which is standard for game development.

title: kaifcodec/user-scanner
summary: A comprehensive OSINT suite for email and username enumeration across 290+ platforms, featuring modular architecture, bulk scanning capabilities, and proxy rotation support.
risk_notes: Installed via pip or nix. The tool performs extensive network requests to third-party websites, which may trigger rate-limiting or security blocks. It supports proxy rotation to mitigate this. As an OSINT tool, it is designed to scrape public data; users should ensure compliance with the terms of service of the target platforms and local privacy regulations.

title: vector-wangel/xlerobot
summary: An open-source, low-cost dual-arm mobile robot platform designed for embodied AI research and home automation tasks, compatible with Raspberry Pi and various controllers.
risk_notes: Requires physical hardware assembly and integration with Python-based control software. Software installation typically involves standard pip/conda environments, but interacting with hardware controllers and cameras requires local system access. No dangerous 'curl | sudo' patterns identified, but users should be cautious when running custom motor control scripts on physical hardware.

title: torchtrade/torchtrade
summary: A modular reinforcement learning framework built on TorchRL designed for both backtesting and live algorithmic trading across multiple exchanges.
risk_notes: Requires Python 3.11+ and uses uv for dependency management. Live trading functionality requires API keys for exchanges (Alpaca, Binance, etc.), which poses a significant financial risk if misconfigured. The framework integrates with LLMs, which may involve sending market data to third-party APIs. Users should exercise caution with automated trading scripts and ensure strict permission management for API keys.

title: nschloe/tiptop
summary: A modern, visually appealing command-line system monitor built with Python and the Textual framework, providing real-time graphs for CPU, memory, and network usage.
risk_notes: Installed via pip, which is standard for Python tools. It requires read access to system statistics via psutil but does not require root privileges for basic monitoring. The project is actively maintained and uses well-regarded dependencies like Textual and psutil.

title: quinnaho/claudemap
summary: ClaudeMap is a visualization tool that uses AI to map codebases into conceptual structures, allowing developers to navigate and understand complex projects through natural language commands.
risk_notes: Installed via npm (npx), which is standard for Node.js tools. It requires access to your local filesystem to analyze code and likely requires an API key for Claude or Codex integration. It is a very new project (created April 2026) with rapid development, so expect potential bugs.

title: arpanghosh8453/open-dronelog
summary: A high-performance, local-first drone flight log analyzer built with Tauri and DuckDB, offering robust data visualization and privacy-focused self-hosting capabilities.
risk_notes: Available as a desktop app (Tauri) or Docker container. The Docker deployment requires standard container permissions. It is local-first, but optional features like DJI API integration require user-provided API keys. The project is actively maintained and provides clear installation paths.

title: verstreubulator/aq-monitor
summary: A comprehensive, well-documented DIY project for building a high-fidelity indoor air quality monitor using ESP32-S3 and ESPHome, fully integrated with Home Assistant.
risk_notes: Requires physical hardware assembly and soldering. Firmware is deployed via ESPHome (standard YAML-based flashing). Requires Home Assistant API credentials and WiFi secrets. No dangerous installation scripts; code is transparent and auditable.

title: iflytek/skillhub
summary: An enterprise-grade, self-hosted registry for managing, versioning, and governing AI agent skill packages with RBAC and audit logging.
risk_notes: Installation involves running a shell script via 'curl | sh' which pulls and executes Docker containers. The application requires persistent storage (S3/MinIO) and handles sensitive API tokens and organizational credentials. It is designed for internal network deployment, but requires careful configuration of OAuth and RBAC to prevent unauthorized access to agent skills.

title: repowise-dev/claude-code-prompts
summary: A collection of high-quality, independently authored prompt templates for AI coding agents, modeled after the behavioral patterns found in Claude Code.
risk_notes: This is a documentation and template repository; it contains no executable code. There is no installation process other than copying text files. It poses zero security risk to your system, though the prompts themselves are intended to be used with AI agents that may have filesystem or shell access.

title: stratequeue/stratequeue
summary: A Python-based CLI tool that bridges the gap between backtesting frameworks and live trading by providing a unified interface for deploying strategies to various brokers.
risk_notes: Installed via pip. Requires handling sensitive financial API credentials (e.g., Alpaca, IBKR) which are stored locally; users must ensure proper environment variable management. The tool executes arbitrary Python strategy scripts, which could perform unauthorized actions if the strategy code is compromised. The project is actively maintained but involves high-risk financial operations.

title: lissy93/web-check
summary: A comprehensive, all-in-one OSINT and website analysis tool that provides deep insights into server architecture, security headers, DNS records, and network infrastructure.
risk_notes: Can be deployed via Docker, which is the recommended and safest method. It performs network-level requests to external domains, so it should be run in an isolated environment if scanning sensitive targets. No root access required for standard operation, but requires network egress permissions.

title: fingerprintjs/fingerprintjs
summary: A widely used, open-source browser fingerprinting library that generates unique visitor identifiers based on browser attributes.
risk_notes: Installed via npm or CDN. As a client-side library, it runs entirely within the user's browser; it does not require server-side secrets or filesystem access, but it is inherently privacy-sensitive and can be blocked by privacy-focused browser extensions.

title: feremabraz/bloomberg-terminal
summary: A high-quality Next.js-based Bloomberg Terminal clone featuring real-time financial data visualization, AI integration, and a sophisticated UI built with shadcn/ui.
risk_notes: Requires Node.js and pnpm for local development. The application relies on external API keys (Upstash Redis, Alpha Vantage, OpenAI) which must be managed via environment variables. It includes built-in security measures like origin restriction and rate limiting, but users should be cautious when deploying to production to ensure API keys are not exposed to the client-side.

title: catchthetornado/text-extract-api
summary: A self-hosted, privacy-focused document extraction and PII anonymization API that leverages local OCR (EasyOCR) and LLMs (via Ollama) to convert PDFs and images into structured JSON or Markdown.
risk_notes: Requires Docker and Ollama. The application runs as a FastAPI service with Celery and Redis; it requires read/write access to the local filesystem for document processing. It is designed for local execution, which mitigates data privacy risks by keeping documents off external cloud services. Maintenance is active, though it relies on a complex stack (Celery/Redis/Ollama) which increases the attack surface if exposed to the network.

title: ark0n/codeman
summary: A web-based dashboard for managing AI coding agents like Claude Code and OpenCode within tmux sessions, featuring a mobile-optimized interface and secure QR-based authentication.
risk_notes: Uses a curl-to-bash installation script which is a common but risky pattern; it requires Node.js and tmux. The application runs as a local web server and interacts directly with your terminal sessions and AI coding agents, meaning it has access to your filesystem and any API keys/credentials configured for those agents. It is actively maintained with recent commits.

title: autonomous-ai/autonomous-computer
summary: A comprehensive open-source guide for building high-performance local AI hardware rigs, including bills of materials, 3D-printable housing files, and software configuration for running large language models.
risk_notes: This is a hardware documentation project, not a software package. Risks are limited to physical assembly and standard OS/driver installation (NVIDIA drivers, Linux/Windows). No automated scripts are provided that require root access or network permissions, but users should follow standard security practices when setting up local model-serving APIs like Ollama or vLLM.

title: yashab-cyber/hackgpt
summary: An enterprise-focused AI penetration testing platform that uses a complex microservices architecture including Docker, Kubernetes, and various monitoring stacks.
risk_notes: The project requires running an 'install.sh' script which typically carries risks of arbitrary code execution. It requires significant infrastructure (Docker, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL, Redis, ELK stack) and handles sensitive security assessment data, requiring careful configuration of API keys and network access to target environments.

title: liam-hq/liam
summary: Liam ERD is a developer tool that automatically generates interactive, visual entity-relationship diagrams from existing database schemas.
risk_notes: Installed via npm (npx @liam-hq/cli init). Requires read access to database schema files or direct database connection strings to generate diagrams. As a CLI tool, it runs in the user's local environment; users should be cautious about sharing sensitive schema information with the hosted web-based visualization service.

title: 2gt-media-group-llc/mikrotik-manager
summary: A self-hosted, full-stack management platform for MikroTik network infrastructure that provides centralized monitoring, configuration, and security auditing.
risk_notes: Deployed via Docker Compose. Requires administrative credentials for all managed MikroTik devices to function. It has full read/write access to your network infrastructure, so it should be isolated in a secure VLAN. The project is in beta (v0.16.2) and actively maintained.

title: hkuds/paper2slides
summary: An AI-powered CLI tool that converts research papers and documents into professional slides or posters using RAG-based content extraction.
risk_notes: Requires local Python environment setup via pip. Needs API keys (likely OpenAI or similar) stored in a .env file to function. Accesses local files for input/output and requires outbound network access to communicate with LLM APIs. Maintenance appears active.

title: showlab/paper2video
summary: An automated pipeline that converts scientific LaTeX papers into presentation videos, integrating slide generation, speech synthesis, and optional talking-head video rendering.
risk_notes: Requires significant local compute resources (recommended NVIDIA A6000 with 48GB VRAM). Installation involves multiple Conda environments and external dependencies like Hallo2. Requires API keys for OpenAI/Gemini, which will have access to any documents processed through the pipeline. The code executes complex pipelines, so reviewing the scripts before running on sensitive documents is advised.

title: inception3d/motion324
summary: Motion324 is a research-oriented framework for 3D motion reconstruction and 4D synthesis, enabling the generation of animated 3D assets from video inputs.
risk_notes: Requires a complex local environment setup using Conda and pip, including compiling custom CUDA-based rasterizers and renderers. It requires significant GPU resources (compute capability > 8.0) and external dependencies like Blender. It uses Wandb for telemetry, requiring an API key. As a research project, it may lack long-term maintenance and contains complex build steps that execute arbitrary code during compilation.

title: andymik90/aperant
summary: Aperant is an autonomous multi-agent framework designed to plan, build, and validate software projects by leveraging Claude's coding capabilities.
risk_notes: The application requires a Claude Pro subscription and local access to your Git repositories. It is distributed as pre-compiled binaries (AppImage, .deb, .dmg, .exe), which requires trusting the maintainer's build pipeline. It operates as an autonomous agent with read/write access to your local filesystem and executes code, which carries inherent security risks; it is recommended to run this in a sandboxed or isolated environment.

title: evolvinglmms-lab/lmms-lab-writer
summary: A local-first, AI-native LaTeX editor built with Tauri that integrates AI agents, Git version control, and automated LaTeX distribution management for academic writing.
risk_notes: Distributed as a native binary (Homebrew/Installer). It requires user-provided API keys for AI features. As a local-first application, it has full access to the user's filesystem within the project directory. The project is actively maintained and uses the secure Tauri framework, which limits the attack surface compared to Electron-based apps.
